One idea to help Delaware Consulting become more sustainable is to reduce business travel and use online meetings more often. Consultants often travel to meet clients. Instead, many meetings and workshops can be done using video calls and online collaboration tools. This will reduce flights and car travel, which helps lower pollution. It also saves time and money. In simple words, doing more work online and less traveling can help the company protect the environment.

One idea to make the company more sustainable is to invest more in inclusive communication and cultural awareness. A respectful and psychologically safe work environment helps build long-term employee well-being, retention, and collaboration. Small remarks or jokes may sometimes be made without harmful intent, but they can still affect how included colleagues from different cultural backgrounds feel. Raising awareness around this could help create a more supportive and sustainable workplace for everyone.. Thanks for all the efforts being put into make the world a better place. Keep it up!

Today, many of us are experimenting with AI in similar ways. We should accelerate the development of shared AI agents that can be reused across teams, so we can scale knowledge better, avoid duplication, and create greater impact faster. This would also have a positive effect on datacenter usage and therefore on the environment, because it would allow us to use AI more efficiently and less fragmented.. delaware should be careful not to apply a classical sustainability model to a business that is not classical in nature.
We are a consulting company focused on digital transformation, not a manufacturing or asset-heavy organization.
Our core activity is delivering services for and with clients, often within their own systems, processes, and working models.
That means our sustainability approach must reflect the reality of client-driven delivery.
At the same time, our business model allows a high degree of remote work.
Physical presence should therefore be used with purpose: for client value, collaboration, team bonding, training, and development.
Our biggest levers are not factories or supply chains, but mobility, hybrid work, digital infrastructure, cloud usage, and AI efficiency.
So the goal should not be to copy a standard framework, but to design one that fits who we are.
A relevant and credible approach will be more effective than a generic one.
In short, our sustainability strategy should be tailored to our business model, our people, and the reality of our client work.
